findings.location: ajax_export_finish() line 161 / ajax_plain_export() line 127 → send_headers() in trait-wc-order-export-ajax-helpers.php line 74
findings.reachability: Authenticated users with view_woocommerce_reports or export capability; nonce-protected
findings.justification: The user-supplied POST settings field 'export_filename' flows through use_ready_or_prepare_settings() and make_filename() without stripping CR/LF characters, then is stored in a transient and later concatenated directly into the Content-Disposition header as filename="$download_name" (helpers trait line 74), enabling HTTP response header injection via newline injection in the filename mask.

findings.reachability: Authenticated admin users viewing order preview; triggered when order data (billing name, address, notes, etc.) contains HTML/JS payloads
findings.justification: ob_get_contents() captures the raw rendered HTML output of build_file() in 'preview' mode (which includes unsanitized order field values for non-plain formats), wraps it in json_encode(), and echoes it; the client-side buttons.js then injects this value via jQuery('#output_preview').html(html) at line 20, executing any script tags present in order data as stored XSS.
